---
title: "Samuel Schapiro"
description-meta: "Interdisciplinary researcher studying the science of intelligence and the creativity of AI systems."
image: "files/images/new_headshot.jpg"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
page-layout: full

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 220px
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:sam@priorcomputers.ai
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/samuel-schapiro
      target: _blank
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/samjschapiro
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=B2UNU6EAAAAJ&hl=en
      target: _blank
---

:::{#hero-heading}
:::

## About

I am an [interdisciplinary scholar](bookshelf.html) studying the science of intelligence and the creativity of AI systems. I'm interested in uncovering the fundamental, scientific principles of creativity governing minds and machines, and in developing AI methods and systems that exhibit creative behavior.

Previously, I was a visiting graduate researcher at the [Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, Berkeley](https://simons.berkeley.edu/homepage), and did my undergrad at the [University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign](https://siebelschool.illinois.edu/), where I studied theoretical computer science.

## Recent news

- **May 2026** — New preprint: [_Assessing the Creativity of Large Language Models: Testing, Limits, and New Frontiers_](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.13450) introduces the DRAT, the first creativity test that significantly predicts LLM scientific ideation.
- **May 2026** — Launched the [Creative AI Index](https://schapiro.ai/creative-ai-index/), a community benchmark for AI creativity.
- **April 2026** — Received the Lambda AI Research Grant.
- **September 2025** — Best Short Paper Award at ICCC 2025 for [_Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory_](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.18687).
- **August 2024** — Started as a visiting graduate researcher at the Simons Institute, UC Berkeley.

## Selected publications

### 2026

- **S. Schapiro**, A. Gladstone, J. Black, H. Ji. [Assessing the Creativity of Large Language Models: Testing, Limits, and New Frontiers](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.13450). Preprint.
- **S. Schapiro**, C. Francisco Park, F. Sosa, L. R. Varshney. [CreativityNeuro: Steering Large Language Model Weights to Improve Divergent Thinking](files/CN.pdf). Under review, Third Conference on Language Modeling (COLM).
- **S. Schapiro**, A. Gladstone, J. Black, H. Ji. [Do Semantic Distance Tests _Actually_ Predict Creativity in Large Language Models?](files/SD_NIPS.pdf) Under review, 40th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS).
- **S. Schapiro**, S. Shashidhar, A. Gladstone, J. Black, R. Moon, D. Hakkani-Tur, L. R. Varshney. [Combinatorial Creativity: A New Frontier in Generalization Abilities](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.21043). Under review, International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR).

### 2025

- **S. Schapiro**, J. Black, L. R. Varshney. [Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.18687). Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC). **Best Short Paper Award.**
- A. Sanyal, **S. Schapiro**, S. Shashidhar, R. Moon, L. R. Varshney, D. Hakkani-Tur. [Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.20090). Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC).

### 2024

- **S. Schapiro**, H. Zhao. [Towards Understanding the Role of Sharpness-Aware Minimization Algorithms for Out-of-Distribution Generalization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.05169). Undergraduate thesis, University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.
